title: Superglobal $_POST
description: Access data sent via HTTP POST.

slides.title: $_POST – Form Data
slides.content:
# The $_POST Superglobal

`$_POST` is an associative array of variables passed to the script via HTTP POST method. Typically used for form submissions.

Example form:
```html
<form method="post" action="process.php">
    <input type="text" name="username">
    <input type="password" name="pass">
    <input type="submit">
</form>
```

In `process.php`:
```php
$user = $_POST['username'] ?? '';
$pass = $_POST['pass'] ?? '';
```

slides.title: Never Trust User Input
slides.content:
## Security Warning

Data from `$_POST` (and `$_GET`) is user-supplied and can be malicious. Always validate and sanitize before using in database queries or outputting to the page.

We'll cover sanitization in the next lesson.
